rows.title: Empty
rows.description: Blank domain. Use right-click menus to add sources, boundaries, and drawn materials.

rows.title: Plane wave in air
rows.description: Atlas 1: homogeneous air with a continuous line source.

rows.title: Plane wave in dielectric
rows.description: Atlas 2: full dielectric domain, n = 1.5, to show shorter wavelength and slower phase velocity.

rows.title: Gaussian pulse in free space
rows.description: Atlas 3: Gaussian pulse in free space with absorbing boundaries.

rows.title: Two-source interference
rows.description: Atlas 4: two coherent point Jz sources separated by about 1.5 lambda0.

rows.title: Two-frequency beat
rows.description: Atlas 5: two nearby CW frequencies to reveal a beat envelope.

rows.title: Single slit diffraction
rows.description: Atlas 6: PEC screen with one subwavelength slit.

rows.title: Double slit
rows.description: Atlas 7: PEC screen with two slits for Young-type interference.

rows.title: Circular aperture
rows.description: Atlas 8: 2D circular aperture cut into a PEC screen.

rows.title: TEz/TMz comparison
rows.description: Atlas 9: TEz/TMz comparison on the same air-dielectric interface, using a dual-polarization angular sweep.

rows.title: Poynting vector
rows.description: Atlas 10: oblique plane wave shown as Poynting flux with vector arrows.

rows.title: Evanescent isolated wave
rows.description: Atlas 11: isolated evanescent wave with explicit k_parallel/k0 > 1 and exponential decay away from the source line.

rows.title: CPML absorption check
rows.description: Atlas 12: pulsed source aimed at the absorbing boundary for reflection checks.

rows.title: Normal air-dielectric interface
rows.description: Atlas 13: air to n = 1.5 vertical interface.

rows.title: Oblique refraction
rows.description: Atlas 14: oblique Gaussian beam on an air-dielectric interface.

rows.title: Brewster TM minimum
rows.description: Atlas 15: p/TM-polarized incidence on an air-dielectric interface near the Brewster angle.

rows.title: TE/TM Brewster comparison
rows.description: Atlas 16: TE/TM Brewster comparison with dual numerical angular sweeps and analytic Fresnel references.

rows.title: Total internal reflection
rows.description: Atlas 17: beam incident from n = 1.5 toward air above the critical angle.

rows.title: Frustrated TIR
rows.description: Atlas 18: two high-index regions separated by a narrow air gap.

rows.title: Quarter-wave coating
rows.description: Atlas 19: air, quarter-wave coating, and n = 1.5 substrate.

rows.title: 1D Bragg mirror
rows.description: Atlas 20: six-pair n = 1.5 / n = 2.5 Bragg mirror.

rows.title: Lossy interface
rows.description: Atlas 21: air to n = 1.5 + 0.1i interface.

rows.title: Anisotropic interface
rows.description: Atlas 22: right-hand medium with eps_x != eps_y.

rows.title: Point electric dipole Jz
rows.description: Atlas 23: localized electric Jz dipole in air.

rows.title: In-plane electric dipole Jx/Jy
rows.description: Atlas 24: localized in-plane electric-current dipole Jx/Jy using the Hz solver.

rows.title: Effective magnetic dipole Mz
rows.description: Atlas 25: localized magnetic Mz dipole using the Hz solver.

rows.title: Dipole over substrate
rows.description: Atlas 26: Jz dipole close to a dielectric substrate.

rows.title: Dipole near PEC mirror
rows.description: Atlas 27: Jz dipole close to a PEC mirror.

rows.title: Huygens source
rows.description: Atlas 28: analytic Huygens-like source for directional radiation.

rows.title: Circular dipole
rows.description: Atlas 29: quadrature circular dipole source.

rows.title: Janus dipole
rows.description: Atlas 30: Janus source near a dielectric waveguide.

rows.title: Equal-phase dipole array
rows.description: Atlas 31: equal-phase dipole array.

rows.title: Progressive-phase dipole array
rows.description: Atlas 32: progressive temporal phase per dipole, with phase step derived from a target steering angle.

rows.title: Slot aperture radiator
rows.description: Atlas 33: source behind a PEC slot aperture.

rows.title: Near-field / far-field NTFF
rows.description: Atlas 34: near-field dipole radiation with normalized equivalence-principle NTFF angular pattern.

rows.title: Single-mode slab waveguide
rows.description: Atlas 35: high-index slab guide excited by a finite-difference fundamental mode profile.

rows.title: Multimode slab waveguide
rows.description: Atlas 36: wider slab guide excited by two finite-difference mode profiles to show multimode beating.

rows.title: Lossy waveguide
rows.description: Atlas 37: lossy high-index guide driven by its finite-difference mode profile.

rows.title: Waveguide bend
rows.description: Atlas 38: 90-degree dielectric waveguide bend using a modal launch into a quarter-ring path.

rows.title: Tapered waveguide
rows.description: Atlas 39: width taper from narrow to wide guide with a modal input field.

rows.title: Width step waveguide
rows.description: Atlas 40: abrupt waveguide width step driven by the input-guide mode.

rows.title: Directional coupler
rows.description: Atlas 41: two parallel guides separated by a small gap, launched from one local guided mode.

rows.title: MMI section
rows.description: Atlas 42: narrow modal input feeding a wider multimode section.

rows.title: Mach-Zehnder interferometer
rows.description: Atlas 43: qualitative Mach-Zehnder interferometer with two dielectric arms and a modal input.
warnings: Description frames the scene as qualitative/proxy; avoid quantitative claims without extra validation.
gaps: no validation-matrix case covers this preset

rows.title: Waveguide with scatterer
rows.description: Atlas 44: small dielectric scatterer beside a mode-launched guide.

rows.title: Microstrip line
rows.description: Atlas 45: qualitative microstrip cross-section with PEC strip, dielectric substrate, and ground plane.
warnings: 2D Hz cross-section proxy for a microstrip-like field pattern, not a calibrated transmission-line model.
gaps: no validation-matrix case covers this preset

rows.title: Stub resonator
rows.description: Atlas 46: side stub attached to a mode-launched straight guide.

rows.title: Fabry-Perot cavity
rows.description: Atlas 47: dielectric cavity placed between two Bragg reflectors.

rows.title: Fabry-Perot standing field
rows.description: Atlas 48: Fabry-Perot resonator driven near a standing-wave condition.

rows.title: Ring resonator
rows.description: Atlas 49: dielectric ring side-coupled to one bus and driven by a near-resonant modal source.

rows.title: Add-drop ring
rows.description: Atlas 50: dielectric ring side-coupled to input/drop buses and driven by a near-resonant modal source.

rows.title: Racetrack resonator
rows.description: Atlas 51: elongated racetrack resonator side-coupled to a mode-launched bus waveguide.

rows.title: Dielectric defect cavity
rows.description: Atlas 52: high-index disk with an internal dipole source.

rows.title: PEC half-wave cavity
rows.description: Atlas 53: PEC half-wave box cavity with a point source.

rows.title: Quarter-wave cavity
rows.description: Atlas 54: shorted quarter-wave stub cavity coupled to a mode-launched dielectric guide.

rows.title: Q by ringdown
rows.description: Atlas 55: pulsed cavity ringdown with an estimated decay Q from the analysis energy trace.

rows.title: Purcell 2D proxy
rows.description: Atlas 56: dipole in a high-index 2D cavity with a comparative Q/Aeff Purcell proxy.
warnings: Description frames the scene as qualitative/proxy; avoid quantitative claims without extra validation.
gaps: no validation-matrix case covers this preset

rows.title: beta-factor guide coupling
rows.description: Atlas 57: dipole coupled to a slab guide with an estimated guided-flux beta factor.

rows.title: Degenerate cavity modes
rows.description: Atlas 58: symmetric disk cavity excited off-center to reveal near-degenerate spectral peaks.

rows.title: PEC cylinder scattering
rows.description: Atlas 59: PEC cylinder under plane-wave illumination.

rows.title: Dielectric cylinder scattering
rows.description: Atlas 60: n = 2 cylinder scattering.

rows.title: High-index Mie cylinder
rows.description: Atlas 61: high-index cylinder for Mie-like resonances.

rows.title: RCS 2D cylinder
rows.description: Atlas 62: PEC cylinder with incident-background subtraction and estimated 2D scattering width.

rows.title: Lossy cylinder
rows.description: Atlas 63: absorbing dielectric cylinder.

rows.title: Dielectric dimer
rows.description: Atlas 64: two high-index cylinders with a narrow gap.

rows.title: Kerker-like 2D scattering
rows.description: Atlas 65: high-index cylinder showing forward/backward scattering contrast with the 2D NTFF observable.

rows.title: Multiple scattering
rows.description: Atlas 66: deterministic random cluster of cylinders.

rows.title: Weak-localization disorder
rows.description: Atlas 67: many weak inclusions for multiple scattering.

rows.title: Anderson-localization disorder
rows.description: Atlas 68: dense deterministic high-contrast disorder for Anderson-localization-like field trapping.

rows.title: Diffusive random medium
rows.description: Atlas 69: extended weak random medium for diffusive transport and speckle-like field structure.

rows.title: Finite-conductivity skin depth
rows.description: Atlas 70: conductive half-space using an explicit J = sigma E update to show skin-depth attenuation.

rows.title: Drude metal
rows.description: Atlas 71: Drude ADE metal with plasma frequency and damping.

rows.title: Lorentz resonant medium
rows.description: Atlas 72: Lorentz ADE resonant dielectric slab.

rows.title: Debye dielectric
rows.description: Atlas 73: Debye relaxation dielectric slab.

rows.title: Plasma cutoff
rows.description: Atlas 74: collisionless plasma slab near cutoff.

rows.title: ENZ slab
rows.description: Atlas 75: passive Drude ADE epsilon-near-zero slab tuned near the source frequency.

rows.title: Anisotropic medium
rows.description: Atlas 76: anisotropic block, eps_x = 4, eps_y = 2.

rows.title: Hyperbolic medium
rows.description: Atlas 77: passive Drude tensor proxy with eps_x > 0 and effective eps_y < 0 near the source frequency.
warnings: Uses a reduced Drude tensor proxy for a hyperbolic medium; acceptable for visualization, not broadband material evidence.

rows.title: 6-field chiral medium
rows.description: Atlas 78: six-component 2D chiral-medium proxy with passivity-limited local magnetoelectric coupling.
warnings: Description frames the scene as qualitative/proxy; avoid quantitative claims without extra validation.

rows.title: 6-field bianisotropic medium
rows.description: Atlas 79: six-component 2D bianisotropic block with anisotropic eps/mu and local magnetoelectric coupling.

rows.title: Gyrotropic medium
rows.description: Atlas 80: qualitative gyrotropic epsilon tensor in the Hz solver, using eps_xy = +g and eps_yx = -g.
warnings: Description frames the scene as qualitative/proxy; avoid quantitative claims without extra validation.

rows.title: 1D Bragg stack
rows.description: Atlas 81: 1D periodic Bragg stack.

rows.title: 2D photonic crystal
rows.description: Atlas 82: square lattice of high-index rods with a reduced plane-wave Bloch-k band estimate.

rows.title: PhC point defect
rows.description: Atlas 83: photonic crystal with one missing central rod, finite-cavity leakage proxy, and reduced Bloch bands.
warnings: Description frames the scene as qualitative/proxy; avoid quantitative claims without extra validation.

rows.title: PhC line-defect waveguide
rows.description: Atlas 84: photonic crystal line-defect waveguide with reduced plane-wave Bloch-k reference.

rows.title: Optimized PhC cavity
rows.description: Atlas 85: L3-like photonic-crystal cavity with locally shifted/tapered rods, Q proxy, and reduced Bloch bands.
warnings: Description frames the scene as qualitative/proxy; avoid quantitative claims without extra validation.

rows.title: Disordered photonic crystal
rows.description: Atlas 86: photonic crystal with deterministic positional disorder.

rows.title: Symmetry-dark mode
rows.description: Atlas 87: symmetric PhC defect excited with an antisymmetric dark-mode source, leakage/Q proxy, and reduced Bloch bands.
warnings: Description frames the scene as qualitative/proxy; avoid quantitative claims without extra validation.

rows.title: Quasi-BIC cavity
rows.description: Atlas 88: weakly symmetry-broken PhC defect showing quasi-BIC leakage with reduced Bloch bands and local asymmetry proxies.

rows.title: Symmetry-protected BIC
rows.description: Atlas 89: symmetry-protected BIC proxy using a symmetric PhC cavity, odd excitation, and reduced Bloch bands.
warnings: Description frames the scene as qualitative/proxy; avoid quantitative claims without extra validation.

rows.title: Fano side resonator
rows.description: Atlas 90: mode-launched straight guide with a side-coupled dielectric resonator.

rows.title: SSH chain, trivial
rows.description: Atlas 91: SSH chain with d1 > d2 and analytic Bloch winding/gap reference.

rows.title: SSH chain, topological
rows.description: Atlas 92: SSH chain with d1 < d2 and analytic Bloch winding/gap reference.

rows.title: SSH interface
rows.description: Atlas 93: interface between two SSH dimerizations with expected edge-state reference.

rows.title: SSH with disorder
rows.description: Atlas 94: SSH interface with deterministic positional disorder and analytic clean-limit Bloch reference.

rows.title: Non-Hermitian SSH
rows.description: Atlas 95: SSH chain with alternating gain/loss, saturable-gain stabilization, and reduced non-Hermitian gap proxy.
warnings: Description frames the scene as qualitative/proxy; avoid quantitative claims without extra validation.

rows.title: Honeycomb lattice
rows.description: Atlas 96: honeycomb-like dimer lattice for valley-photonic demonstrations.

rows.title: Valley Hall interface
rows.description: Atlas 97: two inverted honeycomb domains forming a straight Valley-Hall interface.

rows.title: Valley Hall bend
rows.description: Atlas 98: Valley-Hall interface with a 90-degree bend.

rows.title: Topological pumping
rows.description: Atlas 99: modulated SSH chain showing qualitative Thouless-pump-like transport.
warnings: Qualitative Thouless-pump-like transport proxy; no eigenmode or adiabatic-cycle validation in this pass.

rows.title: Topology with strong defect
rows.description: Atlas 100: Valley-Hall interface with a strong defect placed on the channel.

rows.title: SPP metal-dielectric interface
rows.description: Atlas 101: Drude metal-dielectric interface excited by a near-field dipole.

rows.title: SPP grating coupler
rows.description: Atlas 102: shallow Drude grating for qualitative SPP coupling.
warnings: Description frames the scene as qualitative/proxy; avoid quantitative claims without extra validation.

rows.title: Localized plasmon disk
rows.description: Atlas 103: localized plasmon on a Drude nanodisk.

rows.title: Plasmonic dimer
rows.description: Atlas 104: two Drude disks with a narrow hot gap.

rows.title: Phase-gradient metasurface
rows.description: Atlas 105: phase-gradient metasurface approximated by dielectric bars.

rows.title: Perfect absorber
rows.description: Atlas 106: impedance-matched lossy sheet backed by PEC, with absorptance estimated from line monitors.

rows.title: Negative-index slab
rows.description: Atlas 107: passive Drude ADE epsilon/mu slab tuned to a negative-index band.

rows.title: Drude superlens slab
rows.description: Atlas 108: passive Drude ADE epsilon/mu slab as a qualitative superlens analogue.
warnings: Description frames the scene as qualitative/proxy; avoid quantitative claims without extra validation.

rows.title: Hyperlens
rows.description: Atlas 109: passive dispersive hyperlens analogue using a radial/tangential Drude tensor in the Hz solver.
warnings: Scalar 2D Hz hyperlens analogue; useful for teaching transfer trends, not a full cylindrical 3D validation.

rows.title: Dipole near ENZ slab
rows.description: Atlas 110: dipole close to a passive Drude ENZ slab.

rows.title: Kerr nonlinear slab
rows.description: Atlas 111: instantaneous Kerr slab with epsilon updated from physical field-scaled intensity.

rows.title: SHG χ² slab
rows.description: Atlas 112: chi2 nonlinear slab using a physical field-scaled polarization-current source for second-harmonic generation.

rows.title: THG χ³ slab
rows.description: Atlas 113: chi3 nonlinear slab using a physical field-scaled polarization-current source for third-harmonic generation.

rows.title: Kerr SPM pulse
rows.description: Atlas 114: pulsed Kerr guide/slab for qualitative self-phase modulation and spectral sideband broadening.
warnings: Description frames the scene as qualitative/proxy; avoid quantitative claims without extra validation.

rows.title: Kerr bistable cavity
rows.description: Atlas 115: mode-launched Kerr ring-cavity proxy for optical bistability with an optional bidirectional amplitude sweep.
warnings: Description frames the scene as qualitative/proxy; avoid quantitative claims without extra validation.

rows.title: VO₂ switching slab
rows.description: Atlas 116: VO2-like phase-change slab with intensity threshold, hysteresis, and finite switching time.

rows.title: PCM memory cell
rows.description: Atlas 117: small phase-change memory cell embedded in a mode-launched guide; the switched state persists after the field is cleared.

rows.title: Saturable absorber
rows.description: Atlas 118: bleachable absorber analogue using the phase-state model to reduce loss above threshold.
warnings: Description frames the scene as qualitative/proxy; avoid quantitative claims without extra validation.

rows.title: All-optical switch
rows.description: Atlas 119: mode-launched Mach-Zehnder-like guide with a nonlinear control section for qualitative all-optical switching.
warnings: Description frames the scene as qualitative/proxy; avoid quantitative claims without extra validation.

rows.title: Nonlinear limiter
rows.description: Atlas 120: intensity-triggered lossy phase-change section for qualitative optical limiting.
warnings: Description frames the scene as qualitative/proxy; avoid quantitative claims without extra validation.

rows.title: Temporal interface
rows.description: Atlas 121: finite dielectric interface with nearly uniform temporal epsilon modulation and reduced Floquet sideband S_m estimate.

rows.title: Temporal slab
rows.description: Atlas 122: finite slab with uniform-in-space sinusoidal epsilon modulation and reduced time-refraction sideband S_m estimate.

rows.title: Temporal epsilon modulation
rows.description: Atlas 123: region tagged with sinusoidal epsilon modulation and reduced Floquet sideband analysis.

rows.title: Temporal crystal
rows.description: Atlas 124: extended time-periodic dielectric region with reduced Floquet sideband analysis.

rows.title: Temporally modulated guide
rows.description: Atlas 125: mode-launched slab waveguide with a finite temporally modulated section and reduced S_m sideband readout.

rows.title: Traveling epsilon modulation
rows.description: Atlas 126: guide section tagged with traveling epsilon modulation and forward/reverse transmission check.

rows.title: Temporal isolator analogue
rows.description: Atlas 127: mode-launched traveling-modulation guide section with approximate isolation and reduced Floquet sideband S_m readout.
warnings: Description frames the scene as qualitative/proxy; avoid quantitative claims without extra validation.

rows.title: Modulated ring
rows.description: Atlas 128: mode-launched side-coupled ring resonator with temporally modulated refractive index and sideband S_m estimate.

rows.title: Floquet resonators
rows.description: Atlas 129: mode-launched coupled modulated resonators with reduced Floquet splitting and sideband S_m analysis.

rows.title: Synthetic frequency dimension
rows.description: Atlas 130: mode-launched chain of modulated resonators as a synthetic-frequency analogue with reduced sideband S_m analysis.
warnings: Description frames the scene as qualitative/proxy; avoid quantitative claims without extra validation.

rows.title: PT-symmetric gain/loss guides
rows.description: Atlas 131: PT-symmetric coupled guides with balanced gain/loss, saturable gain, gamma sweep, and reduced modal EP estimate.

rows.title: Exceptional point proxy
rows.description: Atlas 132: PT-symmetric coupled-guide exceptional-point proxy with gamma sweep and reduced 2x2 modal coalescence estimate.
warnings: Description frames the scene as qualitative/proxy; avoid quantitative claims without extra validation.

rows.title: Non-Hermitian skin analogue
rows.description: Atlas 133: non-Hermitian skin-effect analogue using a biased gain/loss SSH chain with reduced skin/localization proxies.
warnings: Reduced active/lossy lattice analogue; not a non-Bloch eigenmode validation.

rows.title: BIC + Kerr
rows.description: Atlas 134: symmetry-protected BIC-style PhC cavity with a Kerr nonlinear defect region, Bloch-k leakage, and active-material overlap proxies.

rows.title: BIC + ENZ
rows.description: Atlas 135: BIC-style PhC cavity side-coupled to a passive Drude ENZ inclusion with Bloch-k leakage and active-material overlap proxies.

rows.title: Janus + topological guide
rows.description: Atlas 136: Janus source coupled into a qualitative Valley-Hall topological guide with reduced guide-coupling proxy.
warnings: Description frames the scene as qualitative/proxy; avoid quantitative claims without extra validation.

rows.title: Huygens + cavity
rows.description: Atlas 137: Huygens source coupled to a dielectric cavity/resonator with reduced cavity-overlap proxy.
warnings: Description frames the scene as qualitative/proxy; avoid quantitative claims without extra validation.

rows.title: Topology + temporal modulation
rows.description: Atlas 138: Valley-Hall channel with a temporally modulated segment, reduced Floquet sideband readout, and coupled-workflow proxies.

rows.title: Nonreciprocity + Valley Hall
rows.description: Atlas 139: Valley-Hall channel with traveling modulation, approximate isolation, reduced sideband S_m readout, and modulation-phase proxy.
warnings: Description frames the scene as qualitative/proxy; avoid quantitative claims without extra validation.

rows.title: Space-time crystal
rows.description: Atlas 140: space-time crystal analogue with traveling epsilon modulation, reduced Floquet sideband readout, and modulation-phase proxy.
warnings: Description frames the scene as qualitative/proxy; avoid quantitative claims without extra validation.
